title: viola adunca subsp. oxyceras
intro: Viola adunca subsp. oxyceras, also known as the bear's-ear violet, is a subspecies of perennial plant that belongs to the violet family (Violaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Viola adunca subsp. oxyceras include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its possible antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
